mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals weakening labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power, which directly translates into lower corporate earnings expectations and thus negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60% of periods where UNRATE is rising on a 3-month average basis.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls to extremely low levels, it signals aggressive monetary easing aimed at stimulating the economy. This action eventually lowers the discount rate for future cash flows, supporting asset prices and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in more than 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS is below 0.5% for at least two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales and a rise in the unemployment rate indicate a severe contraction in consumer demand and confidence. This dual pressure directly impacts corporate revenues and profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average change < -1% AND UNRATE 3-month average change > 0.5%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 40% of triggered instances despite the combined negative signal from retail sales and unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: In a recessionary regime, a sharp and sustained decline in industrial production signals a broad contraction in manufacturing and overall economic activity. This directly translates into lower corporate earnings and thus negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances when UNRATE is above 7%.

mechanism_narrative: A significant contraction in housing starts indicates a severe downturn in the construction sector and broader economic weakness. This negatively impacts related industries and overall market sentiment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in more than 55% of periods where HOUST is below 500 (3-month average).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained and significant job losses, as indicated by declining non-farm payrolls, signal a weakening labor market and reduced consumer purchasing power. This ultimately pressures corporate revenues and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 45% of periods where PAYEMS has declined by more than 500,000 over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Extremely low inflation combined with very low interest rates suggests a deflationary environment and weak demand. This can lead to a liquidity trap and prolonged economic stagnation, negatively impacting corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances despite the combined negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A significant contraction in both industrial production and retail sales signals a broad and severe economic downturn. This reduces corporate profitability and investor confidence across sectors, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 40% of triggered instances when both INDPRO and RSAFS show significant declines.
